articles.title: Nasdaq 100 sinks 5% in AI-led rout as yields climb
articles.description: Growing anxiety about valuations sent the S&P 500 down 2.6%, with the index failing to complete a 10th straight week of gains. The Nasdaq 100 sank about 5%, the most since April 2025.

articles.description: State-run miner NDTV approves Rs 3,000 crore Vizag blending facility as it looks to move beyond volume growth.

articles.description: The automobile company claimed that it will invest around ₹150 crore in the two projects as part of its broader strategy to strengthen renewable energy use and reduce dependence on fossil fuels

articles.title: Trump urges new acting spy chief Bill Pulte to fire intelligence employees
articles.description: Trump had earlier said in an interview with the Wall Street Journal that he wanted Pulte -- who will oversee the 18 sprawling US intelligence agencies -- to slash employee numbers.

articles.title: India-US trade pact first phase likely by mid-July
articles.description: India and US set to finalize first phase of bilateral trade deal by mid-July after positive talks, aiming for preferential access and cooperation.

articles.title: PSU Banks Added More Branches. But Employee Growth Didn't Keep Up
articles.description: Major state-run lenders reported lower employee-to-branch ratios between FY22 and FY26 while outlining plans to automate processes, expand digital banking and deploy AI tools.

articles.title: India defies West Asia war concerns as Q4 GDP growth hits 7.8%; risks remain ahead
articles.description: India's economy surged by a better-than-expected 7.8% in the March quarter, exceeding forecasts and pushing FY26 growth to 7.7%. This robust performance was driven by strong private investment and consumption, defying concerns over the West Asia war. The government remains committed to its reform agenda to maintain economic momentum.

articles.title: Leo Horoscope Today, June 6, 2026: A practical decision may unlock a rewarding new chapter
articles.description: Leo Horoscope Today: A promising development in money, work, or relationships may bring greater stability and steady progress today.

articles.title: Reform, perform, transform will go on: PM Modi hails 7.7% GDP growth
articles.description: Addressing a public meeting in Daman near Gujarat, the PM said India would continue to move on the path of “reform, perform and transform” at a fast pace. Modi said, “Today, a good news has come... The figures that have come today make it clear how strong the foundation of India’s economy is.”

articles.title: India’s FY26 growth hits 7.7%
articles.description: Indian economy grew at 7.7% in FY 2025-26, with GDP reaching 7.8% in Q4. Despite challenges, reforms and hard work are driving growth momentum.

articles.title: Chip slump erases $1.3 trillion in stock market value
articles.description: The PHLX's combined loss of 12% over two sessions shows investors are becoming more concerned about pricey, high-flying tech stocks just as Elon Musk prepares a blockbuster initial public ⁠offering next week for SpaceX at an exceedingly high $1.75 trillion valuation.

articles.title: GDP rose 7.7% in FY26, 7.8% in Q4; RBI trims this year's projection to 6.6%
articles.description: India's economy surged by 7.8% in the Jan-March quarter, driven by strong investment, consistent farm output, and expansion in construction and tertiary sectors. This robust demand offset potential negative impacts from West Asian conflicts. The government remains committed to driving economic momentum through decisive policy measures.

articles.title: From tax waivers to free hedges, RBI & govt join hands to boost Rupee
articles.description: Government and RBI have launched new measures to attract foreign investment in bonds and bank deposits. These initiatives aim to boost inflows by easing regulations and offering currency protection. The rupee strengthened significantly following the announcements, with potential for substantial retail deposit growth. Tax exemptions for foreign investors on government securities are also in place.

articles.title: $2 Trillion Wiped Out In Hours: What Triggered Bloodbath In US Tech Stocks?
articles.description: On Wall Street, all three major indexes ended lower, led by heavy selling in technology shares. Dow Jones fell 1%, the S&P 500 lost 2.4%, while the Nasdaq Composite plunged 4%.

articles.title: Could this new spirulina finally give vegans the vitamin B12 they have been waiting for? Scientists say this breakthrough may change plant-based nutrition forever
articles.description: For years, vitamin B12 has remained one of the biggest challenges in vegan nutrition. Now, a breakthrough in active B12 spirulina could change that. Scientists produced bioavailable vitamin B12 levels comparable to beef while maintaining spirulinas high-protein profile. If scaled successfully, this sustainable superfood may reshape plant-based diets, food security, and future protein production worldwide.

articles.description: India's central bank holds interest rates steady despite rising inflation. The focus shifts to stabilizing the rupee with a new dollar deposit scheme. This move aims to attract foreign currency and boost confidence. While offering gains to non-resident Indians, it comes at a significant cost and distorts market rates.

articles.title: SBI Mutual Fund buys stakes in 2 Adani group firms for ₹5,747 cr
articles.description: SBI Mutual Fund on Friday bought stakes in Adani Enterprises and Adani Energy Solutions from US-based GQG Partners for Rs 5,747 crore through open market transactions. SBI Mutual Fund purchased 1,64,39,984 shares representing nearly a 1.3 per cent stake in Adani Enterprises, the flagship entity of the Adani Group, according to block deal data on the National Stock Exchange (NSE). In addition, SBI MF also acquired 63,65,796 shares, amounting to a 0.52 per cent stake in Adani Energy Solutions. The shares were picked up in the price range of Rs 1,504.80-2,913.40 apiece, taking the combined transaction value to Rs 5,747.55 crore. Meanwhile, Rajiv Jain-backed GQG Partners, through its affiliate GQG Partners Emerging Markets Equity Fund, offloaded the same number of shares in the two Adani group companies. The stake buy comes after SBI Mutual Fund last month acquired a 0.45 per cent stake in Adani's flagship firm Adani Enterprises, for Rs 1,435 crore. Shares of Adani Enterprises rose 2

articles.title: Coal linkage auctions: Coal India steps up supply to non-regulated sector, offers record volumes
articles.description: Coal India Limited is introducing new measures to increase coal supply for non-regulated sector consumers. This aims to reduce import dependence on high gross calorific value coal and meet growing industrial needs. Record coal will be offered in linkage auctions targeting the sponge iron sector. Steel producers gain flexibility to sell coal byproducts. New projects can secure coal linkages before commissioning.

articles.title: RBI holds repo rate at 5.25%: What it means for home loan borrowers and homebuyers
articles.description: The RBI has kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% for the fourth consecutive MPC meeting, ensuring that home loan borrowers continue to benefit from lower borrowing costs. The decision is expected to support housing demand, improve affordability, and provide stability to both homebuyers and the real estate sector.

articles.title: Gas Prices Today, June 5: Check fuel latest rates in California, Texas, Florida, New York, and Pennsylvania as National Average sees a dip
articles.description: Gasoline prices nationwide have seen a welcome dip, with the average price of regular fuel dropping by 18 cents to $4.24 per gallon. This decline, attributed to crude oil staying below $100 a barrel, offers some relief for summer travelers. However, ongoing concerns about the Strait of Hormuz's reopening cast a shadow of uncertainty over future fuel costs.

articles.title: LIC buys Maruti Suzuki shares worth Rs 68 crore, insurer's stake in automaker crosses 5%
articles.description: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) has increased its stake in Maruti Suzuki above the 5% threshold after purchasing 51,750 shares through a market transaction. The move comes amid a decline in Maruti Suzuki's stock this year and reflects LIC's continued investment activity despite market volatility.

articles.title: Hindustan Zinc Shares Sink 6% As Govt Weighs 2% Stake Sale Worth Up To Rs 5,000 Crore
articles.description: Hindustan Zinc shares fell nearly 6 percent as the government weighs selling up to a 2 percent stake, aiming to raise about Rs 5000 crore as part of its disinvestment drive

articles.title: 300 billion barrels: Venezuela has more oil than Saudi Arabia. Why did it collapse?
articles.description: Much of its reserves are located in the Orinoco Belt and consist of heavy crude. Unlike the lighter crude found in many Middle Eastern fields, heavy oil is more expensive and technically challenging to produce and refine.

articles.title: India GDP data today: Q4 growth likely above 7 per cent; FY27 outlook faces oil, global risks
articles.description: Economists expect India to remain the fastest-growing major economy, with Q4 FY26 growth estimates ranging between 7.1-7.3 per cent. However, projections indicate growth could moderate in FY27 amid geopolitical tensions and external uncertainties.

articles.title: Silver prices to remain under pressure unless oil price declines: Analyst
articles.description: In near-term, the metal is expected to be under pressure unless oil prices come down on sustainable basis
